behaviors.description: The generated function signature matches the expected interface (parameter names, types, return type) specified in the description.
behaviors.justification: Failure depends on real LLM output quality (it may choose wrong names/types/return). A stubbed model would hide that. Rejected Workflow: behavior only needs one model decision, not a multi-agentic chain. Rejected Integration: text targets signature correctness of generated code itself, not just routing/parsing logic.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real generation call for a prompt with a strict expected signature, parse the generated function signature, and assert exact match on parameter names/types and return type.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Adversarial critique: if production has deterministic post-generation signature normalization/enforcement, an Integration test could be enough; behavior text does not state such enforcement, so Agentic remains minimum.

behaviors.description: Orchestration executes attempts in order and returns immediately on the first passing attempt.
behaviors.justification: This is module orchestration/state-handoff logic across generator, tester, and attempt controller; deterministic stubs can expose ordering/short-circuit bugs. Rejected Unit: risk is mainly at module boundaries. Rejected System/Agentic/Workflow: no real OS/network dependency and no need for real model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Wire orchestrator with stub generator outputs and stub test-runner results (fail then pass), assert call order by attempt index and that no subsequent attempt is executed after first pass.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Adversarial critique: if orchestration is a single pure function with no boundary bugs, Unit might suffice; behavior wording emphasizes execution flow across attempts, so Integration is still the safer minimum.

behaviors.description: For a function that a single call usually fails, the live iterative loop can still produce a passing implementation within the allowed attempts.
behaviors.justification: Requires multiple real LLM-driven iterations (generate/refine) plus intervening test feedback, so correctness depends on sequential agentic decisions. Rejected Agentic: one-shot model test does not validate the multi-step loop behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the full live loop on a known function where first-attempt pass rate is low, allow up to 3 attempts, and assert at least one run reaches a passing implementation within limit.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Adversarial critique: if second attempt is forced via deterministic template patching (not model decision), Agentic could suffice; behavior explicitly says live iterative loop producing pass, which implies true multi-step model involvement.

behaviors.description: When tests fail, the refinement prompt builder includes the failed test names and failure messages in the next LLM prompt.
behaviors.justification: Primary failure is data handoff from test results into prompt construction for the next call; this is cross-module wiring and formatting, catchable with deterministic fixtures. Rejected Unit: a pure prompt-template test can miss schema/handoff mismatches. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no need to validate model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run one failed attempt with synthetic test failures through real test-result parser + prompt-builder integration, then assert the next prompt contains each failed test name and associated failure message.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Adversarial critique: if prompt builder is directly fed already-normalized failure objects by contract, Unit may be enough; behavior references 'next LLM prompt' after test failure, so boundary coverage is still required.

behaviors.description: The next live attempt changes implementation content in response to prior failure context rather than resubmitting the same code.
behaviors.justification: Needs at least two real model steps (initial + refined) with failure context carried across; a stub can trivially 'change' output and hide whether real model actually responds to context. Rejected Agentic: single-call evaluation cannot validate context-driven evolution across attempts.
behaviors.test_description: Run live attempt 1 to failure, capture failure context, run attempt 2 with refinement prompt, and assert attempt-2 code differs meaningfully from attempt-1 code and aligns with at least one cited failure.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Adversarial critique: if test only checks string inequality, random non-semantic drift could pass; keep Workflow but require context-linked change assertions to avoid false positives.

behaviors.description: The service stops after 3 failed attempts and returns a structured error with all three attempts and their test results.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic control-flow + aggregation across orchestrator, attempt store, and result formatter. Stubs for generation/test failure are sufficient to expose off-by-one and payload-shape bugs. Rejected System: no required real OS/network behavior in the behavior text. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no dependence on model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Inject three deterministic failing attempts via stubs, run orchestrator, assert it halts exactly at 3 and returns a structured error containing all attempt artifacts and test-result details.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Adversarial critique: if production failure metadata is only available through real subprocess runner output, System may be needed; behavior text only requires structured aggregation after failures, so Integration is still justified.

behaviors.description: The full non-agentic pipeline wiring (generate call, test execution, attempt bookkeeping) works end to end with deterministic stubbed model outputs.
behaviors.justification: Even with stubbed model outputs, 'full pipeline wiring' including test execution typically crosses real subprocess/filesystem boundaries; in-process fakes can hide runner/OS integration failures. Rejected Integration: behavior explicitly asks end-to-end wiring including execution layer.
behaviors.test_description: Use deterministic model stubs but run the real code-execution/test-runner path (actual temp files/process invocation), then assert attempt bookkeeping and pass/fail propagation are correct end to end.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Adversarial critique: if test execution is purely in-process (no subprocess/OS boundary), Integration would be enough; behavior says full pipeline test execution, so System is the conservative minimum.

behaviors.description: The full live loop (generate, test, refine, retest) solves at least one known hard function that fails in the first attempt.
behaviors.justification: This is explicitly a multi-step live agentic pipeline with sequential model decisions and feedback loop; correctness depends on end-to-end iterative behavior, not one call. Rejected Agentic: single-step agentic test cannot verify loop-level success dynamics.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real generate-test-refine-retest loop on a benchmark hard function with documented first-attempt failure, and assert the loop reaches a passing solution within max attempts.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Adversarial critique: if 'known hard function' is too easy for current model, pass may occur on attempt 1 and not exercise loop; retain Workflow but choose a case with stable first-attempt failure evidence.
